data.title: Flight Centre Updates on Ongoing Share Buy-Back Program
data.description:
Flight Centre Travel Group Limited ( (AU:FLT) ) just unveiled an update.

Flight Centre Travel Group Limited has announced an update regarding its ongoing on-market buy-back program. As of October 3, 2025, the company reported the buy-back of 22,163 ordinary fully paid securities on the previous day, adding to the total of 8,486,661 securities bought back previously. This move is part of the company’s strategy to manage its capital structure and potentially enhance shareholder value.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:FLT) stock is a Buy with a A$15.10 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Flight Centre Travel Group Limited stock, see the AU:FLT Stock Forecast page.

Flight Centre Travel Group Limited operates in the travel industry, providing travel services and products. The company focuses on offering comprehensive travel solutions, including booking and travel management services, catering to both individual and corporate clients.

data.description:

data.title: Surge in US Vehicle Sales Boosts Auto Stocks
data.description: The actual sales figure surpassed analyst estimates of 16.2 million units, suggesting stronger-than-expected consumer demand. This positive surprise is likely to boost automotive stocks, as higher sales volumes can lead to increased revenues for car manufacturers. The impact on the stock market may be short-term, driven by sentiment, but it also hints at a resilient consumer sector, which could influence broader market expectations.

data.title: DigiCo Infrastructure REIT Announces Major Customer Wins and Updated Earnings Guidance
data.description:
The latest update is out from Digico Infrastructure REIT ( (AU:DGT) ).

DigiCo Infrastructure REIT has announced significant customer wins across its Australian data centre platform, leading to an increase in contracted IT capacity to 41MW by June 2026. This expansion is primarily driven by demand from Hyperscale, Neocloud, Enterprise, and Government customers. The company is accelerating the expansion of its SYD1 site to meet this demand, with revenue from these wins expected to phase in over the second half of FY26. DigiCo has updated its financial guidance for FY26, projecting an underlying EBITDA of $120-125 million and growth capital expenditure of $160-180 million. The company is well-positioned to capture growth in high-density AI infrastructure, supported by its strategic status under the Australian Government hosting certification framework.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:DGT) stock is a Buy with a A$3.90 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Digico Infrastructure REIT stock, see the AU:DGT Stock Forecast page.

More about Digico Infrastructure REIT

DigiCo Infrastructure REIT (DGT) is a diversified owner, operator, and developer of data centres with a global portfolio. The company has a broad investment mandate across Stabilised, Value-add, and Development opportunities, with a portfolio consisting of 13 data centres in key Australian and North American markets. The REIT plans for 238MW of IT capacity, including 76MW of installed capacity and a 162MW development pipeline.

Average Trading Volume: 2,362,197

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: A$1.5B

See more insights into DGT stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Idaho Strategic Resources Reduces Sales Commission in New Agreement
data.description:
Idaho Strategic Resources ( (IDR) ) just unveiled an update.

On September 29, 2025, Idaho Strategic Resources, Inc. entered into a Sales Agreement with Roth Capital Partners, LLC, reducing the sales commission from 3% to 2.5%. This agreement allows the company to offer and sell its common stock through the Sales Agent, with the potential to raise up to $20 million, impacting the company’s market operations and financial strategy.
